title: End-to-end token usage and cost tracking (L4 > transport-mux > SDK > L6)
issueSummary: Token usage is parsed in agent-core but omitted from AgentCorePromptResult; transport-mux exposes usage/cost data but does not feed SDK COST_TRACKED journal events; SDK can aggregate cost events but receives no live transport feedback; agent-platform has session budget APIs but orchestration does not enforce auto-pause.
triageComment: The full chain to build is: agent-core session returns token usage in AgentCorePromptResult; transport-mux proxy feeds normalized cost records to the SDK journal; agent-platform enforces session cost budgets and auto-pauses when exceeded. Related: #575, #587, #599, #604.
constraints: Do not expand scope to full streaming/history work from #575 except where needed to avoid double-counting streaming final usage events.
Do not rely on token estimation fixes from #587 for authoritative provider usage; preserve provider-reported usage when available.
Keep COST_TRACKED events backward-compatible with existing SDK cost journal consumers and old journals without cost events.
Avoid tight coupling between the generic transport-mux proxy and SDK storage; prefer injectable metadata and feedback sinks.
Do not write prompt text, raw credentials, or unsanitized provider payloads into cost events.
Budget auto-pause must happen at orchestrator-safe checkpoints and include an explicit budget reason.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentCorePromptResult exposes optional normalized usage metadata for OpenAI/Azure and Anthropic direct API calls.
Transport-mux emits normalized cost feedback for non-streaming completions and exactly once for streaming completions with final usage.
SDK appends COST_TRACKED events containing provider/model, input/output/cache tokens, effect identity when available, and an idempotency key or duplicate guard.
SDK run cost stats and effect index include the journaled cost data without regressing replay of journals that lack cost events.
Agent-platform updates session cost state from run/effect cost data, emits threshold alerts once, and auto-pauses safely when a configured budget is exceeded.
A deterministic integration test proves a mocked completion/effect produces one COST_TRACKED event and triggers budget handling when over limit.
